title: Motion In a Straight Line Test - 2 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: The displacement of a particle moving in a straight line is described by the relation, $s = 6 + 12t - 2t^2$. Here $s$ is in metre and $t$ in second. The distance covered by particle in first 5 s is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A point moves with uniform acceleration and $v_1$, $v_2$ and $v_3$ denote the average velocities in the three successive intervals of time $t_1$, $t_2$ and $t_3$. Which of the following relations is correct?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle has an initial velocity of $9\ \mathrm{m/s}$ due east and a constant acceleration of $2\ \mathrm{m/s^2}$ due west. The distance covered by the particle in the fifth second of its motion is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Which of the following four statements is false?
options: A body can have zero velocity and still be accelerated.
A body can have a constant velocity and still have a varying speed.
A body can have a constant speed and still have a varying velocity.
The direction of the velocity of a body can change when its acceleration is constant.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body with constant acceleration travels 2 metre in the first 2 second and 2.2 m in the next 4 second. The velocity at the end of the seventh second from the start shall be
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Four points A, B, C, and D lie along a straight line such that the distances between consecutive points are equal ($AB = BC = CD$). An object travels along this path with constant acceleration. If the average velocities over the intervals AB and CD are $12\ \mathrm{ms}^{-1}$ and $20\ \mathrm{ms}^{-1}$ respectively, determine the average velocity for the segment BC.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Mark the correct statement for a particle going on a straight line

(A) If the velocity and acceleration have opposite sign, the object is slowing down.

(B) If the position and velocity have opposite sign, the particle is moving towards the origin.

(C) If the velocity is zero at an instant, the acceleration should also be zero at that instant.

(D) If the velocity is zero for a time interval, the acceleration is zero at any instant within the time interval.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle has an initial velocity $\vec{u} = (6 \hat{i} + 8 \hat{j}) \text{ ms}^{-1}$ and an acceleration of $\vec{a} = (0.8 \hat{i} + 0.6 \hat{j}) \text{ ms}^{-2}$. Its speed after 10 s is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The acceleration is constant when the relationship between
options: the square of the position coordinate $x$ and the velocity $v$ is linear
the position coordinate $x$ and the reciprocal of the velocity is linear
the position coordinate $x$ and velocity $v$ is linear
the position coordinate $x$ and the square of the velocity $v$ is linear
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two particles start moving from the same point along the same straight line. The first moves with constant velocity $v$ and the second with constant acceleration $a$. During the time that elapses before the second catches the first, the greatest distance between the particles is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is moving so that its displacement is given as $s=t^3-6 t^2+3 t+4$ meter. Its speed at the instant when its acceleration is zero will be (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Between two stations a train accelerates uniformly at first, then moves with a constant speed and finally retards uniformly. If the ratios of the time taken are $1: 8: 1$ and the greatest speed attained by the train is $60 \mathrm{kmh}^{-1}$, find the average speed, in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$, over the whole journey.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A train leaves station $A$ from rest. It moves with constant acceleration $a_1$ over a certain part of the track, and then moves with constant retardation $a_2$ over the remaining part so that it finally stops at station $B$. The distance between $A$ and $B$ is 4 km, and the total time taken for the journey is $1 / 15 \mathrm{~h}$. Taking accelerations in km per minute unit, prove that $\frac{1}{a_1}+\frac{1}{a_2}=x$. Find the value of $x$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a car race, car $A$ reaches the finishing line 4 s earlier than car $B$ and crosses the finish with a speed that is $v$ more than that of car $B$. Suppose both cars begin from rest and move with uniform accelerations $a_1=4 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ and $a_2=1 \mathrm{~ms}^2$ respectively. Determine the value of $v$ in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: As a train accelerates uniformly it passes successive kilometre marks while travelling at velocities of $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ and then $10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. Determine the train's velocity, in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$, when it passes the next kilometre mark and the time it takes, in s, to travel the 2 km distance.
questions.subject: Physics
